Fixed: injected GA4 (G-D020NJ3FCC, same property as the landing) into the shared blog template head, so all 1,669 blog pages now report. OWNER-ONLY follow-ups (honesty rule — I won't fabricate): (1) re-shoot a few app screenshots from a lived-in Android account (current F-series have minor typos/empty-streak); (2) a named founder quote + real Play Store rating to slot into the credibility/trust strip when available.

The dedicated section was technical (Claude/GPT/Gemini cards with jargon like 'long-context reasoning', 'multimodal recall'). Now it's benefit-first: heading 'One coach. Also softened the other prominent name-drops: hero chip 'Claude·GPT·Gemini' -> 'Powered by frontier AI'; stat label 'Frontier models — Claude, GPT, Gemini' -> 'AI models working as one coach'; feature-band stat -> benefit copy. A normal fitness user now reads what it DOES for them, not which models run it. Kept the poetic H1 'Three frontier AI models. One serious athlete.' as brand voice — tell me if you'd rather change that too. Live on nyus.in. NYUSLANDING@f079f65.

issues.description: Added everything still error, add the error in this red error message so that we log and fix it

fix.description: Done — the real Google sign-in error is now logged + surfaced so we can fix the root cause. Before: the handler showed a generic 'Try again' and deliberately discarded the raw error. Now on failure it (1) captureException()s the real error to Sentry with {feature:'google_signin', raw}, (2) console.error logs the full message+code, and (3) the red toast shows the ACTUAL reason (e.g. '[16] Account reauth failed', capped 120 chars) instead of a generic line — so the next failure is diagnosable on-screen and in Sentry. src/pages/Welcome.tsx, tsc clean, App commit b577f321 (ships next alpha). NOTE: the live Google-failure state can't be force-triggered in the dev browser (the web path gates to the email flow when no OAuth client is configured), so the attached shot is the login surface; the fix is the error capture/surface path, verified by code + typecheck. Next real failure will now show + log the cause.

App commit b05453e2 (ships in next alpha build).

issues.description: This page is not scrolling or scrollable on mobile

fix.description: Root cause: 'Your plan is ready' (PlanReadyOverview) used MobilePage variant=scroll, which relies on a route-level scroller — but OnboardingFlow mounts the custom builder/overview inside a 'fixed inset-0 flex flex-col' container with no scroller, so content past the viewport (PLAN DETAILS + the Continue button) was clipped and the page wouldn't scroll on mobile. Fix: variant=fixed heightMode=fill → self-contained overflow-y-auto scroll region. Verified on the builder: 2950px content scrolls in the 844px column, 'Continue to dashboard' reachable at the bottom. App commit b05453e2 (ships in next alpha build).
